name: Poppy Seed Ravioli Cookies
recipeInstructions:
Mix sugar, shortening, margarine (or butter), eggs, and vanilla. Stir in flour, baking soda, and salt. Divide dough into 4 equal parts. Cover and refrigerate for 2 hours.
Place poppy seeds, almonds, milk, honey, lemon peel, and lemon juice in a blender or food processor. Blend until the liquid is absorbed.
Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Roll one part of dough into a rectangle, 12 x 8 inches, on a lightly floured surface. Cut dough into 12 rectangles, each 3 x 2 inches. Place a teaspoon of poppy seed mixture on one end of each rectangle. Carefully fold the dough over the filling using a metal spatula or knife dipped in flour. Seal edges by pinching them together. Press edges with a fork dipped in flour. Place cookies on an ungreased cookie sheet. Bake for 8 to 10 minutes, or until lightly browned. Brush warm cookies with honey and sprinkle with poppy seeds. Remove from rack and cool. Repeat with remaining dough. Makes 4 dozen cookies; each cookie contains approximately 95 calories.
